title: Franklin County Domestic  and Juvenile Court

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any internal audit reports, statistical reviews, or emails generated by Court Administration or the IT Department comparing the extreme disparity in cases terminated via "Unavailability of party for trial" between specific judges. Specifically, any records analyzing, discussing, or evaluating why Judge Lasheyl Stroud reported 578 "Unavailability" terminations from January 2023 to March 2026, while Judges Kim Browne, Douglas Nobles, and James Brown each reported fewer than 20 during the identical reporting period.

2. A digital database export or spreadsheet identifying the case numbers for all 'Marriage Terminations with Children' assigned exclusively to Judge Lasheyl Stroud that were placed on 'Inactive', 'Stay', or 'Unavailability of party for trial' status between January 1, 2023, and the present date. This request is specifically limited to identifying only those cases where the aforementioned status was officially entered on the docket between the 12th and 18th month after the initial case filing.

3. Any written policies, emails, internal memorandums, or directives issued by Judge Lasheyl Stroud instructing her specific magistrates, bailiff, or staff attorneys to place cases on the inactive docket, issue stays, or classify cases as "Unavailable for trial" for the purpose of tolling the Supreme Court of Ohio’s 18-month time guideline.

4 Any digital communications involving Judge Stroud or her bailiff discussing the "30-day guideline" for Domestic Violence cases and the use of dismissals to prevent cases from appearing as "overage".

5. Any written reports, notifications, emails, or memorandums submitted to Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins by, or on behalf of, Judge Stroud pursuant to Sup.R. 40(B), identifying or explaining any motions pending for more than 120 days, for the period of October 1, 2024, through March 31, 2026.

6. All written complaints, grievances, or reports whether internal (from employees, court staff) or external (from litigants, counsel, witnesses, members of the public) concerning the conduct of Magistrate Terrence Scott, including but not limited to allegations of harassment, hostile work environment, retaliation, or inappropriate professional conduct.

7. All records of any internal investigation, inquiry, or review conducted by the Court, its administrative office, human resources, or outside counsel in connection with such complaints, including investigative reports, witness interview summaries, findings, and recommendations.

8. All correspondence, including email, letters, text messages, teams messages, and memoranda,  among the Court's judges, administrators, HR personnel, and counsel concerning complaints, investigations, or disciplinary action regarding Magistrate Terrence Scott. 

9. Any internal announcements concerning the removal, termination, or resignation of Magistrate Terrence Scott.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Doe
